position: absolute; height: 24.0039px; width: 9.0625px; left: 0px; top: 0px;"><div data-processed="true" style="inset: 0px; opacity: 0.001; overflow: hidden; position: absolute; user-select: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"></math></div></div></span></span>&nbsp;sequences and shared epitopes. This causes models to overfit on specific known pairs rather than learning the universal chemical rules of recognition. This project investigates if we can move beyond sequence identity to predict&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Neoantigen</span>&nbsp;specificity.<span class="txxDge notranslate" data-sfc-root="c" data-wiz-uids="Gsskzb_14,Gsskzb_15" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="visibility: hidden;"><span class="vKEkVd" data-animation-atomic="" data-wiz-attrbind="class=Gsskzb_13/TKHnVd" data-sae="" style="text-wrap-mode: nowrap; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS" style="font-size: 14px;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Residue Pair Encoding (RPE):</span>&nbsp;Transforming sequence pairs into&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">2D interaction matrices</span>. This represents the spatial and chemical relationship between residues, helping the model identify binding&nbsp;<em class="eujQNb" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: inherit;">mechanisms</em>&nbsp;rather than just sequence strings.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEICRAB" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS" style="font-size: 14px;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">CNN Architecture:</span>&nbsp;Implementing Convolutional Neural Networks designed to detect local clusters of high-affinity interactions within the RPE matrix.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEICRAC" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; Data Preprocessing</span><br></font><ul style=""><li style=""><span style="background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));"><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b style="">Binary Labeling: </b>Standardises the dataset into two classes, mapping Neoantigen as 1 and all other disease categories as 0.</font></span></li><li style=""><span style="background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));"><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b>Dictionary Mapping: </b>Creates a lookup table for all 441 possible amino acid pairs (including an 'X' token) to assign each interaction a unique integer ID.</font></span></li><li style=""><span style="background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));"><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b>Residue-Pair Encoding:</b> Generates a 25x25 interaction matrix for every TCR-Epitope pair by calculating the specific ID for every residue combination.</font></span></li><li style=""><span style="background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));"><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b>Sequence Normalization: </b>Truncates or Zero-Pads sequences to a fixed length of 25, replacing non-standard amino acids with an 'X' token for consistency.</font></span></li><li style=""><span style="background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));"><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b>Matrix Transformation:</b> Converts the categorical sequence data into numerical Integer Matrices, representing the 2D spatial relationship of the binding site.</font></span></li><li style=""><span style="background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));"><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b style="">Optimized Storage:</b> Exports the final tensors as .npy files and saves the encoding maps as JSONs to ensure fast, reproducible model loading.</font></span></li></ul></div><div><br></div><div class="otQkpb" aria-level="3" role="heading" data-animation-nesting="" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" data-sae="" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; RPE Dataloader</font></div><div class="otQkpb" aria-level="3" role="heading" data-animation-nesting="" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" data-sae="" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; line-height: 28px; color: rgb(10, 10, 10); background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"><ul><li><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b style="">Custom Dataset Class (PairMatrixDataset):</b> Efficiently loads the pre-processed .npy interaction matrices and binary labels into a PyTorch-compatible format.</font></li><li><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b>Tensor Conversion: </b>Dynamically transforms 25x25 NumPy arrays into LongTensors, representing the unique residue-pair IDs for model input.</font></li><li><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b>Mask Generation: </b>Produces a fixed-length Binary Mask for each TCR-Epitope pair to support future attention-based or sequence-length operations.</font></li><li><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b>Structured Data Fetching: </b>Returns a dictionary for each sample containing the interaction matrix, the padding mask, and the binary target (Neoantigen vs. Others).</font></li><li><font style="font-size: 15px;" face="Comic Sans MS"><b style="">Batch Management: </b>Utilises the PyTorch DataLoader to handle automated shuffling and batching, ensuring high-performance data streaming to the GPU.</font></li></ul></div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="340" width="730" x="80" y="1960" break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS" style="font-size: 15px;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Residue Embedding Layer</span>: learn chemical similarities between different amino acid interactions.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIAhAB" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS" style="font-size: 15px;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Spatial Feature Extraction (CNN Step 1)</span>: Uses a&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">2D Convolutional layer</span>&nbsp;to scan the 25x25 interaction matrix, capturing local structural patterns between the TCR and Epitope.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIAhAC" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS" style="font-size: 15px;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Multi-Scale Feature Learning</span>: Implements&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Parallel 1D Convolutions</span>&nbsp;with varying kernel sizes (1, 3, 5, and 7) to detect binding motifs of different lengths simultaneously.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIAhAD" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS" style="font-size: 15px;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Dimensionality Reduction</span>: Employs&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Max-Pooling layers</span>&nbsp;and Dropout to reduce the sequence length while retaining the most prominent activation signals and preventing overfitting.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIAhAE" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS" style="font-size: 15px;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Temporal Dependency (LSTM)</span>: Processes the extracted features through&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Stacked LSTM layers</span>&nbsp;to capture long-range dependencies and the sequential nature of the binding interface.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIAhAF" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS" style="font-size: 15px;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Global Feature Fusion</span>: Combines multi-scale and recurrent features into a unified vector via&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Masked Max-Pooling</span>, ensuring the model ignores padded positions.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIAhAG" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Classification Head</span>: Passes the final 256-dimensional feature vector through a&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Linear layer</span>&nbsp;to produce the final prediction for&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Neoantigen vs. Others</span>.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIAhAG" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><font color="#0a0a0a" face="Comic Sans MS"><span style="font-size: 15px;"><b>Non-Linear Activation (ReLU): </b>Applied after every convolution and batch normalization step to introduce non-linearity, allowing the model to learn the complex chemical rules of TCR-epitope binding.</span></font></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIAhAG" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><font color="#0a0a0a" face="Comic Sans MS"><span style="font-size: 15px;"><b>Gradient Flow Optimization:</b> Mitigates the "vanishing gradient" problem during backpropagation, ensuring the network's 1.5 million parameters start; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"><font face="Comic Sans MS">This project demonstrates that&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Residue Pair Encoding (RPE)</span>&nbsp;combined with a&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Hybrid CNN-LSTM</span>&nbsp;architecture effectively identifies cancer-specific TCR signatures. By transforming sequence data into 2D interaction matrices, the model achieved a high&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">AUC of 0.883</span>, proving it can distinguish&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Neoantigens</span>&nbsp;from other disease categories with significant accuracy.<span class="txxDge notranslate" data-sfc-root="c" data-wiz-uids="WEGL4d_r,WEGL4d_s" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="visibility: hidden;"><span class="vKEkVd" data-animation-atomic="" data-wiz-attrbind="class=WEGL4d_q/TKHnVd" data-sae="" style="text-wrap-mode: nowrap; position: relative;"><span aria-hidden="true"></span><button tabindex="0" data-amic="true" data-icl-uuid="a6c24bef-29a3-4d49-a794-c5b76e405810" aria-label="View related links" class="rBl3me" data-wiz-attrbind="disabled=WEGL4d_q/C5gNJc;aria-label=WEGL4d_q/bOjMyf;class=WEGL4d_q/UpSNec" data-ved="2ahUKEwiFkp6Z4ceTAxVxaEEAHWtvESIQye0OegYIAQgCEAE" data-hveid="CAEIAhAB" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: rgb(10, 10, 10); text-align: start; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"><font face="Comic Sans MS">The model showed exceptional&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">sensitivity</span>, capturing&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">95% of Neoantigens</span>&nbsp;(40 out of 42) in the test set. While the&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">F1 Score graph</span>&nbsp;indicated a degree of overfitting due to recurring TCR structures in cancer datasets, the results confirm that the network successfully learned&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">non-linear structural motifs</span>&nbsp;rather than simple sequence identity. This work establishes a robust framework for cross-chain disease prediction and advances our understanding of the universal chemical rules governing TCR specificity.<span class="txxDge notranslate" data-sfc-root="c" data-wiz-uids="WEGL4d_z,WEGL4d_10" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="visibility: hidden;"><span class="vKEkVd" data-animation-atomic="" data-wiz-attrbind="class=WEGL4d_y/TKHnVd" data-sae="" style="text-wrap-mode: nowrap; line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: rgb(10, 10, 10); text-align: start; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"><font face="Comic Sans MS">The&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">T-Cell Receptor (TCR)</span>&nbsp;is a membrane-bound heterodimer (typically Alpha and Beta chains) that acts as the primary sensory organ for the adaptive immune system. Its sole biological function is to scan&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Peptide-MHC (pMHC) complexes</span>&nbsp;on the surface of other cells.&nbsp;</font><span style="font-family: &quot;Comic Sans MS&quot;; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600;">2. CDR3β vs. CDR3α: The Dominant Sensor</span></div><div class="Y3BBE" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIBRAA" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-size: 16px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));">While both chains contribute to the binding interface, the&nbsp;</span><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-family: &quot;Comic Sans MS&quot;; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); font-weight: 700;">CDR3β (Complementarity Determining Region 3 beta)</span><span style="font-family: &quot;Comic Sans MS&quot;; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));">&nbsp;loop is the functional "epicentre" of antigen recognition:</span></div><div class="Y3BBE" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIBxAA" data-complete="true" data-processed="true" style="font-size: 16px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><font face="Comic Sans MS"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Sequence Redundancy &amp; Overfitting</span>: Cancer datasets often contain "public" TCRs (sequences shared across patients). A model can easily "memorize" these specific strings (pattern matching) rather than learning the&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">universal chemical laws</span>&nbsp;of how amino acids interact.</font></span></li><li class="dF3vjf" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-hveid="CAEIDhAB" data-complete="true" data-sae="" style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><font face="Comic Sans MS"><span class="T286Pc" data-sfc-cp="" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">Chain-Dependency</span>: Most models are "chain-locked." A model trained on Beta chains typically fails to generalize to Alpha chains, even though the fundamental&nbsp;<span class="Yjhzub" data-sfc-root="c" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="font-weight: 700;">residue-pair chemistry</span>&nbsp;governing the interaction remains identical across both.</span><span class="txxDge notranslate" data-sfc-root="c" data-wiz-uids="zltMye_2x,zltMye_2y" data-sfc-cb="" data-complete="true" style="visibility: hidden;"><span class="vKEkVd" data-animation-atomic="" data-wiz-attrbind="class=zltMye_2w/TKHnVd" data-sae="" style="text-wrap-mode: nowrap; span.style: font-weight: 700; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start;
span.font: RPE-Net: Evaluating Residue Pair Encoding for Disease Prediction using TCR(CDR3beta AA)

switch.text: RPE-Net: Evaluating Residue Pair Encoding for Disease Prediction using TCR(CDR3beta AA)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 743px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1605px; margin-left: 50px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
margin-block: 24px 12px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 28px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

button.style: margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(233, 235, 240), rgb(33, 35, 39)); border-width: initial; border-style: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(86, 89, 94), rgb(159, 161, 166)); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);

div.style: line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
div.font:
The Problem: Generalization vs. Memorization
TCR-epitope binding models often suffer from "data leakage" because cancer datasets frequently contain redundant CDR3
sequences and shared epitopes. This causes models to overfit on specific known pairs rather than learning the universal chemical rules of recognition. This project investigates if we can move beyond sequence identity to predict Neoantigen specificity.

div.style: line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
div.font:
The Data
Utilizing a curated subset of the McPAS-TCR database, the project transitions to a binary classification task:

ul.style: margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; line-height: 24px; padding-inline-start: 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

span.font: Target: Neoantigen-associated TCRs.
Control: "Others" (Infectious disease or Autoimmune TCRs).

font.span: Structure: Curated CDR3–Epitope–Disease triplets across multiple cancer types.

button.style: margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(233, 235, 240), rgb(33, 35, 39)); border-width: initial; border-style: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(86, 89, 94), rgb(159, 161, 166)); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);

div.style: line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
div.font:
The Approach
To force the model to learn structural patterns rather than just "remembering" sequences, we use:

ol.style: margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; line-height: 24px; padding-inline-start: 20px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

span.font: Residue Pair Encoding (RPE): Transforming sequence pairs into 2D interaction matrices. This represents the spatial and chemical relationship between residues, helping the model identify binding mechanisms rather than just sequence strings.
CNN Architecture: Implementing Convolutional Neural Networks designed to detect local clusters of high-affinity interactions within the RPE matrix.

Robust Evaluation: Testing the model’s ability to distinguish Neoantigens from Others, specifically monitoring for overfitting caused by high-frequency CDR3
structures shared across different cancer types.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 288px; height: 1px; padding-top: 2105px; margin-left: 252px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 683px; height: 1px; padding-top: 2440px; margin-left: 61px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
margin-block: 24px 12px; line-height: 28px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

span.style: font-weight: 600; font-size: 18px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));
background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));
span.font: Binary Labeling: Standardises the dataset into two classes, mapping Neoantigen as 1 and all other disease categories as 0.

span.style: background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));
span.font: Dictionary Mapping: Creates a lookup table for all 441 possible amino acid pairs (including an 'X' token) to assign each interaction a unique integer ID.

span.style: background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));
span.font: Residue-Pair Encoding: Generates a 25x25 interaction matrix for every TCR-Epitope pair by calculating the specific ID for every residue combination.

span.style: background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));
span.font: Sequence Normalization: Truncates or Zero-Pads sequences to a fixed length of 25, replacing non-standard amino acids with an 'X' token for consistency.

span.style: background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));
span.font: Matrix Transformation: Converts the categorical sequence data into numerical Integer Matrices, representing the 2D spatial relationship of the binding site.

span.style: background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));
span.font: Optimized Storage: Exports the final tensors as .npy files and saves the encoding maps as JSONs to ensure fast, reproducible model loading.

div.style: margin-block: 24px 12px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 28px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 728px; height: 1px; padding-top: 3295px; margin-left: 51px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
margin-block: 24px 12px; line-height: 28px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
font-weight: 600; margin-block: 24px 12px; line-height: 28px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
margin-block: 24px 12px; line-height: 28px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

li.font: Custom Dataset Class (PairMatrixDataset): Efficiently loads the pre-processed .npy interaction matrices and binary labels into a PyTorch-compatible format.
Tensor Conversion: Dynamically transforms 25x25 NumPy arrays into LongTensors, representing the unique residue-pair IDs for model input.
Mask Generation: Produces a fixed-length Binary Mask for each TCR-Epitope pair to support future attention-based or sequence-length operations.
Structured Data Fetching: Returns a dictionary for each sample containing the interaction matrix, the padding mask, and the binary target (Neoantigen vs. Others).
Batch Management: Utilises the PyTorch DataLoader to handle automated shuffling and batching, ensuring high-performance data streaming to the GPU.

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(86, 89, 94), rgb(159, 161, 166)); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);

ul.style: margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; line-height: 24px; padding-inline-start: 16px; text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;

span.font: Residue Embedding Layer: Maps integer pair IDs into a 128-dimensional continuous space, allowing the model to learn chemical similarities between different amino acid interactions.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;

span.font: Spatial Feature Extraction (CNN Step 1): Uses a 2D Convolutional layer to scan the 25x25 interaction matrix, capturing local structural patterns between the TCR and Epitope.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;

span.font: Multi-Scale Feature Learning: Implements Parallel 1D Convolutions with varying kernel sizes (1, 3, 5, and 7) to detect binding motifs of different lengths simultaneously.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;

span.font: Dimensionality Reduction: Employs Max-Pooling layers and Dropout to reduce the sequence length while retaining the most prominent activation signals and preventing overfitting.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;

span.font: Temporal Dependency (LSTM): Processes the extracted features through Stacked LSTM layers to capture long-range dependencies and the sequential nature of the binding interface.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;

span.font: Global Feature Fusion: Combines multi-scale and recurrent features into a unified vector via Masked Max-Pooling, ensuring the model ignores padded positions.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;

span.font: Classification Head: Passes the final 256-dimensional feature vector through a Linear layer to produce the final prediction for Neoantigen vs. Others.

font.span: Non-Linear Activation (ReLU): Applied after every convolution and batch normalization step to introduce non-linearity, allowing the model to learn the complex chemical rules of TCR-epitope binding.
Gradient Flow Optimization: Mitigates the "vanishing gradient" problem during backpropagation, ensuring the network's 1.5 million parameters continue to learn effectively across deep layers.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 693px; height: 1px; padding-top: 5370px; margin-left: 49px;

span.font: 4. Training & Evaluation Pipeline

button.style: margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(233, 235, 240), rgb(33, 35, 39)); border-width: initial; border-style: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(86, 89, 94), rgb(159, 161, 166)); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);

ul.style: margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; padding-inline-start: 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

span.font: Reproducibility Control: Fixes random seeds across Python, NumPy, and PyTorch to ensure identical results and consistent model weights across multiple runs.
Optimization & Loss: Employs the AdamW optimizer and Cross-Entropy Loss to iteratively update the network’s 1.5 million parameters and minimize prediction errors.
Performance Monitoring: Calculates a robust suite of metrics, including F1-Macro and Recall, to measure the model's accuracy in distinguishing Neoantigens from other categories.
Automated Checkpointing: Tracks test performance at the end of every epoch and automatically saves the optimal model state to prevent the use of overfitted versions.
Evaluation Pipeline: Feeds unseen test data through the trained network to generate probability scores and final class labels for unbiased performance validation.
Visual Analytics: Generates ROC curves and Loss/F1 graphs to provide a clear visual history of the model’s learning progress and its discriminative power.
Results Serialization: Exports final predictions and performance graphs to CSV and image files for formal project documentation and clinical review.

font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
div.font: This project demonstrates that Residue Pair Encoding (RPE) combined with a Hybrid CNN-LSTM architecture effectively identifies cancer-specific TCR signatures. By transforming sequence data into 2D interaction matrices, the model achieved a high AUC of 0.883, proving it can distinguish Neoantigens from other disease categories with significant accuracy.

div.style: font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
div.font: The model showed exceptional sensitivity, capturing 95% of Neoantigens (40 out of 42) in the test set. While the F1 Score graph indicated a degree of overfitting due to recurring TCR structures in cancer datasets, the results confirm that the network successfully learned non-linear structural motifs rather than simple sequence identity. This work establishes a robust framework for cross-chain disease prediction and advances our understanding of the universal chemical rules governing TCR specificity.

div.font: The T-Cell Receptor (TCR) is a membrane-bound heterodimer (typically Alpha and Beta chains) that acts as the primary sensory organ for the adaptive immune system. Its sole biological function is to scan Peptide-MHC (pMHC) complexes on the surface of other cells.

span.style: font-family: "Comic Sans MS"; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600;

div.span: 2. CDR3β vs. CDR3α: The Dominant Sensor

div.style: font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

div.span: While both chains contribute to the binding interface, the

font-family: "Comic Sans MS"; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229));

div.span: loop is the functional "epicentre" of antigen recognition:

div.style: font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

button.style: margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(233, 235, 240), rgb(33, 35, 39)); border-width: initial; border-style: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;

ul.style: margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; padding-inline-start: 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

span.font: Hypervariability: Due to V(D)J recombination, the Beta chain possesses significantly higher combinatorial diversity than the Alpha chain, providing a broader repertoire for recognizing rare mutations.

font.span: Structural Centricity: Crystallographic data consistently shows the CDR3β loop positioned centrally over the peptide-binding groove. If the Alpha chain provides the structural scaffold, the Beta chain provides the high-definition "fingerprint" that dictates binding specificity.

button.style: margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(233, 235, 240), rgb(33, 35, 39)); border-width: initial; border-style: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;

div.style: font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
div.font: By analyzing the specific amino acid sequence of the CDR3β in conjunction with the Epitope (peptide), we are essentially modeling the biophysical handshake of the immune response.

ul.style: margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; padding-inline-start: 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

span.font: The Mechanism: We use these two sequences to predict the "binding event"—the likelihood that this specific TCR will trigger a T-cell response against a specific disease.

font.span: The Target: In this project, we focus on distinguishing Neoantigens from other peptides, allowing us to identify T-cells that are natively equipped to fight cancer.

button.style: margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: light-dark(rgb(233, 235, 240), rgb(33, 35, 39)); border-width: initial; border-style: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;

div.style: font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: 12px 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
div.font: Predicting these interactions is notoriously difficult due to two main hurdles:

ul.style: margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; padding-inline-start: 16px; color: light-dark(rgb(10, 10, 10), rgb(229, 229, 229)); text-align: start; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));

span.font: Sequence Redundancy & Overfitting: Cancer datasets often contain "public" TCRs (sequences shared across patients). A model can easily "memorize" these specific strings (pattern matching) rather than learning the universal chemical laws of how amino acids interact.

font.span: Chain-Dependency: Most models are "chain-locked." A model trained on Beta chains typically fails to generalize to Alpha chains, even though the fundamental residue-pair chemistry governing the interaction remains identical across both.
